name: Sengen Sama (mountain goddess)
shortDescription: Sengen Sama is a Japanese mountain goddess, particularly associated with Mount Fuji. She is revered as a protector and embodiment of the sacred mountain.
description: Sengen Sama, also known as Asama no Kami, is a prominent Japanese mountain goddess deeply connected with Mount Fuji. She is worshipped as a protector and embodiment of the mountain's spiritual power. Her origins are complex, blending indigenous Shinto beliefs with Buddhist influences. While often considered a Shinto deity, her association with Mount Fuji, a site of Buddhist pilgrimage and asceticism, integrates her within a broader Buddhist context. Sengen Sama is believed to control volcanic activity and ensure the mountain's continued well-being. Pilgrims and worshippers pray to her for safety, good fortune, and blessings from the sacred mountain. The Sengen Shrine network, dedicated to her worship, is primarily located around Mount Fuji, solidifying her connection to the landscape. The deity's significance extends to the arts, inspiring countless depictions of Mount Fuji throughout Japanese history. Offerings to Sengen Sama often include sake, rice, and prayers for protection from natural disasters, highlighting her role as a guardian figure in Japanese folklore.

sources.title: Encyclopedia of Shinto
sources.author: Institute for Japanese Culture & Classics, Kokugakuin University
